title: Marsdenia thouarsii
taxonomy: Marsdenia thouarsii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It belongs to the genus Marsdenia, which consists of evergreen or deciduous vines and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. Marsdenia thouarsii is native to Madagascar, where it is found in humid forests at high elevations. The classification of Marsdenia thouarsii is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its succulent leaves and large, showy flowers. Marsdenia thouarsii is also known to be closely related to other species within the genus Marsdenia.

intro: Marsdenia thouarsii, also known as the Madagascar Milk Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The Madagascar Milk Vine is used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Marsdenia thouarsii include alkaloids, such as marsdenine and thouarsine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Marsdenia thouarsii include teas and infusions, as well as extracts in capsule or powder form, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and asthma.
